finding_results.notes: The exposure now uses the canonical CMU / Raj Patel / sequential reasoning benchmarks version throughout the reveal and the later on-screen callback.
The recap now matches the continuity sheet by setting Marcus's server-room lockout to Day 4.
The chapter keeps the eighteen-body pre-Ahmad baseline and the final list now includes Arvind so Ahmad cleanly makes nineteen.
Ahmad and Jun-seo are now seeded before the killing, Jun-seo's fixation on Kyung-hee is explicit, and Ahmad is established as the exhausted person trying to keep him functional.
The paragraph now registers cold and rot through Noor's body and one smell detail, without restating the half-degree cooling mechanism or listing composite smells.
Priya no longer repeats the full visa/mother/chemo/Delhi inventory and instead frames the pressure as ongoing home-relay stakes.
The counting-metacommentary is gone. The passage keeps only the reconciled nineteenth-body list.
The fabricator, institution, research area, and Noor's location all align to the CMU / Raj Patel / sequential reasoning benchmarks / Pittsburgh version cited in the packet and continuity sheet.
Kenji is no longer named as a Day 3 corpse in this chapter. The pod now belongs to Arvind, removing the contradiction with Kenji's later on-page survival.
Heading contract is preserved and the revised chapter is 3590 words.

Broke the balanced thesis parallel by separating Priya's accusation (line 167) from her self-confession (line 179), which now arrives as an unexpected pivot rather than a neat 'you did X / I did Y' block. Recovered the snapshot's productive roughness with the 'I could not —' sentence fragment (line 179), consistent with Priya's style-bible stress profile where sentences fragment only under extreme duress. Added Noor's provocative 'It was smaller' (line 181) to create a live exchange that triggers Priya's composure break at 'Do not tell me that' (line 183). Inserted a scene-local trigger (scraping from the lab, line 183) as a physical interruption between speech blocks. Chemo and visa now land concretely ('She is in chemotherapy in Delhi. My visa depends on this position.') but as separate declarations after Priya's composure has already shifted, not as a unified thesis summary. Restored Noor's interrupted line 'The analysis doesn't depend on —' (line 187) from the pre-revision snapshot. Removed the too-neat 'Neither do I' mirror that closed the original exchange. Priya's low contraction level preserved throughout (do not, could not, cannot); Noor's high contraction level maintained (Yeah, doesn't, don't).

finding_results.notes: Reworked the fragment-heavy opening, corridor observation beats, Tomás exchange, and postmortem tally into grammatically complete close-third sentences. Pressure now comes from Noor's exact observation and sentence variation rather than stacked auxiliary-less noun phrases.

pass_summaries.summary: Aligned Noor's exposed scandal to the CMU/Raj Patel canon, repaired the Day 4 lockout and nineteenth-body arithmetic, replaced the Kenji contradiction with Arvind, expanded the Ahmad/Jun-seo fallout so the humiliation lands as a specific social break, and brought the chapter to 3590 words.

Single finding addressed: Priya's corridor confrontation reworked to break the thesis cadence while preserving all must-land facts (falsified citations, chemo in Delhi, visa dependency, credibility collapse). The balanced moral-comparison block was split so Priya's self-confession arrives as an unplanned pivot from her accusation sequence rather than a polished parallel. A scene-local trigger, a sentence fragment under duress, and a provocative exchange between the characters replace the composed position-paper rhythm. Voice separation and contraction levels maintained per style bible.

Revised chapter 13 in place for the prose/copyedit pass, eliminating the packet's repeated thriller-fragment rhythm in the opening, Tomás beat, and nineteen-dead tally while preserving the chapter's humiliation engine, survivor reactions, and ending state.
